seo.title: CI/CD for AI Apps implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping CI/CD for AI Apps projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for validating CI/CD pipelines used in AI application development. It focuses on the specific challenges of non-deterministic outputs, prompt versioning, and cost-effective evaluation cycles.

items.title: Decouple Prompts from Source Code
items.description: Store prompt templates in dedicated JSON or YAML files separate from application logic to enable independent versioning and testing.

items.title: Implement Schema Validation
items.description: Run JSON schema validation against prompt templates in the CI pipeline to ensure all required variables are present before deployment.

items.title: Map Prompts to Specific Model Versions
items.description: Hardcode specific model versions (e.g., gpt-4-0613) in configuration files rather than using 'latest' aliases to prevent unexpected drift during deployments.

items.title: Automated Prompt Diffing
items.description: Configure the CI pipeline to generate and display a diff of prompt changes in pull request comments for manual reviewer audit.

items.title: Metadata Tagging
items.description: Inject the Git commit SHA and environment name into the metadata of all LLM API calls for downstream traceability.

items.title: Unit Test LLM Wrappers
items.description: Use mocks for LLM API calls in unit tests to verify that application logic handles various response formats and error codes without incurring costs.

items.title: Structured Output Verification
items.description: For prompts intended to return JSON, execute a validation step in CI that parses the response and checks for required fields and data types.

items.title: Semantic Similarity Testing
items.description: Run an evaluation script comparing LLM outputs against a 'golden set' of expected answers using cosine similarity or LLM-as-a-judge metrics.

items.title: Regression Testing Suite
items.description: Maintain a dataset of 50-100 edge-case prompts that must be executed and validated whenever the model version or system prompt changes.

items.title: Prompt Injection Scanning
items.description: Integrate a security scanner in the pipeline to test if system prompts can be bypassed via malicious user input patterns.

items.title: Environment Secret Management
items.description: Store LLM API keys in a secure vault (e.g., GitHub Secrets, HashiCorp Vault) and ensure they are never logged in CI build traces.

items.title: Health Check Endpoints
items.description: Implement a dedicated /health/ai endpoint that verifies connectivity to upstream AI providers and model availability before routing traffic.

items.title: Blue-Green Deployment for Prompts
items.description: Configure the deployment pipeline to route a small percentage of traffic to new prompt versions before full cutover.

items.title: Rollback Automation
items.description: Define automated rollback triggers based on a spike in 4xx/5xx errors or a drop in semantic similarity scores post-deployment.

items.title: Inference Latency Benchmarking
items.description: Measure and log the p95 latency of model responses during integration tests to prevent performance regressions from reaching production.

items.title: CI Token Usage Caps
items.description: Set hard limits on the number of tokens or API spend allowed per CI pipeline run to prevent runaway costs from recursive loops or large test suites.

items.title: Evaluation Parallelization
items.description: Distribute LLM evaluation tasks across multiple parallel CI jobs to reduce build times when running large-scale semantic tests.

items.title: Model Weight Caching
items.description: If self-hosting models, use persistent volume claims or CI caching layers for model weights to avoid multi-gigabyte downloads on every build.

items.title: Conditional Evaluation Execution
items.description: Configure CI to only run expensive LLM evaluation suites if changes are detected in prompt files or model configurations.

items.title: Small Model Testing
items.description: Use smaller, cheaper models (e.g., GPT-3.5 or Haiku) for basic logic testing in CI, reserving larger models for final staging evaluations.

items.title: OpenTelemetry Instrumentation
items.description: Ensure the CI/CD pipeline verifies that OpenTelemetry spans are correctly configured for all LLM calls to enable production tracing.

items.title: Log Masking Verification
items.description: Run a test case in CI that attempts to log PII through the AI feature and verifies that the logging middleware correctly redacts the data.

items.title: Metric Dashboard Deployment
items.description: Automate the deployment of Grafana or Datadog dashboards alongside the application to track token usage and error rates per model.

items.title: Feedback Loop Capture
items.description: Verify that the production environment is configured to capture and store user 'thumbs up/down' feedback linked to specific prompt versions.

items.title: Alerting Thresholds
items.description: Verify that deployment scripts update alerting thresholds for inference failures and token exhaustion in the monitoring provider.
